title: Latches and Flip-Flops
summary: Covers synchronous bistable circuits: RS, D, JK, and T latches (level-active), the master-slave D flip-flop (edge-active), and their applications.
content_points: Latches add a synchronisation (clock) input to the basic RS bistable.
D latch: Q_{t+1} = D regardless of previous state; no forbidden combination.
JK latch: J=K=1 inverts current state instead of being forbidden.
T latch: Q_{t+1} = Q_t XOR T (toggle on T=1).
Flip-flop: edge-active – state changes only on the rising/falling clock edge; makes exactly one step per clock period.
Master-slave: two latches in series clocked in antiphase – effectively edge-triggered.
Use flip-flops for control; latches for asynchronous circuits.
learning_objectives: Describe and compare RS, D, JK, T latches
Distinguish when to use latches vs. flip-flops
